page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.3xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions, and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA extensions. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates consistent mid-tier performance. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1531.0 and a multi-core score of 7760.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 16209.01. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 633.64 GB/sec, which drops to 76.48 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The CPU's vector extensions support specialized workloads, as evidenced by a Geekbench ray tracing score of 13335.0 and a software compilation score of 11558.0. For database operations, the instance handles 11.74 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a Passmark database score of 5802.95.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high compute-to-RAM density, the ecs.c7.3xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, processing 222.56 text generation tokens per second on a small 135M model. However, its performance degrades significantly on larger models, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model dropping to a weak 23.52 tokens per second, indicating it is not suitable for large-scale LLM tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 24.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark results place its performance in the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7760.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 16209.01. It delivers strong throughput for static web serving at 15.7 GB/sec and handles 11.74 million Redis SET operations per second. The instance is highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, offering a balanced, cost-efficient resource density for dedicated CPU tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for CPU-intensive workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core compute-optimized instance designed for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and lightweight vector-accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
AVX-512 and VNNI support for vector math and cryptography
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and Redis databases
